text: while.” “Maybe during the second movie I’ll show my appreciation by returning the favor.” “Now that you’ve put it out there, I’m going to hold you to it.” Frank closed the car door and started across the lot in a daze. He couldn’t believe that he’d just gotten a blowjob in his car in a public arena from his wife of nearly ten years. It was the kind of thing they would have done in their younger days, and in fact it left Frank feeling more youthful than he had in years. He glanced at the screen, realizing he hadn’t paid the slightest attention to the movie since it had started. James Dean was up there being all moody and angst-ridden, winning the affection of his brother’s girl. This was actually Frank’s favorite James Dean picture, but he didn’t regret missing the beginning. The show in his Camry had been much more engrossing. As he neared the concession stand, Frank’s nose prickled as the sharp odor of gasoline assaulted his nostrils. He stopped in his tracks and looked around until he spotted the source. A Plymouth Savoy with Belvedere trim sat in a spot close by, and there must have been a crack in the gas tank because it was dribbling fuel in a puddle that was starting to run out from under the car in a small river. Concerned, Frank stepped up to the car to alert the driver, but he peered through the half-opened window with the speaker affixed to it to find the vehicle empty. He took a moment to admire the restoration job to this classic; even the leather of the seats smelled fairly new. Frank stepped back and scanned the immediate area but saw no one that looked as if they might belong to this car. Glancing uneasily at the spreading puddle of gas, he hurried on to the concession stand. There were only two kids in line in front of him so he didn’t have to wait long. He ordered a large tub of buttered corn and two small sodas, noting that the 50s theme extended to ridiculously low prices for the food and beverages. Before leaving with the goodies, he pointed back over his shoulder and said to the pimply faced boy working the counter, “There’s a car leaking gas back there.” The boy’s eyes were wide with a haunted quality, and Frank was suddenly afraid he was going to cry. Instead the boy dropped his head, staring down at the sticky countertop, and said in a voice so soft and breathy that Frank really had to strain to make out the words, “It’s always leaking. There’s nothing we can do to stop it.” Frank didn’t know how to respond to that so he just gathered up his items and started back to the car. As he approached the Plymouth he saw that there was now a young man leaning against the back of it with his left foot propped on the tire. He wore a red jacket not unlike the one worn by Dean in Rebel , jeans so tight they were surely killing off any chance he had to father children in the future, his pompadour impressive in its height. He looked to be eighteen or nineteen, a look of practiced indifference on his face. Someone whose idea of cool was to seem as if he didn’t care whether or not he looked cool. The cigarette he puffed on completed the picture. “Hey,” Frank called out, noticing that the young man was standing with his right foot in the puddle of gasoline but apparently oblivious to it. “Watch out! You’ve got a gas leak!” The boy glanced at Frank, the cigarette dangling from his lips. Frank noticed how the boy’s trembling mouth made the cigarette shake and jitter. The boy sprung a leak himself, a few tears trailing down his cheek, ruining his “too cool for school” vibe. “I know,” he said. “Happens every night. It’s my fault, always my fault. Maybe this is my punishment.” Confused, Frank opened his mouth to tell the boy to get the hell away from the gas with that cigarette, but at that moment a perky redheaded girl whose pink sweater was working overtime to constrain her ample bosom walked up to the boy in the “Rebel” jacket. Natalie Wood to his James Dean. The young man smiled with one corner of his mouth even as tears continued to trickle down his face then, without looking down, flicked the still-burning cigarette to the ground. To Frank, it seemed the butt fell in slow motion, reality stretching out like silly putty. He thought maybe he tried to shout a warning but he heard no sound. He was frozen, watching the events unfold as if this was just a movie up on the screen. The cigarette landed and ignited the gas instantly, a large whoosh of flame that engulfed the young man’s right leg. He certainly lost his cool then, letting loose with a scream so high-pitched Frank was surprised the windows of all the nearby cars didn’t shatter. The girl in the too small sweater also screamed and back-peddled quickly, tripping over her feet and toppling into the dirt. Time resumed its regular course at that point…or, it seemed to Frank, perhaps it speeded up a bit. He dropped the snacks and started forward to try to help the young man, but then the Plymouth caught fire and the gas tank exploded. The force knocked Frank onto his back, and the young man’s screams ended…only to be replaced by a multitude of screams throughout the lot. Frank pushed up on his elbows and looked back toward the Plymouth…or at least where the Plymouth had been. The explosion had apparently sent it airborne and the burning hulk of the car was now resting upside down on top of a Ford Thunderbird.
